description: Subnet Without Its Own Flow Log (Covered Only at VPC Level)
rationale: This subnet has no flow log of its own, but its parent VPC has a VPC-level flow log, which already captures traffic for every subnet in the VPC. Network traffic for this subnet is therefore being logged, so this is not a gap in coverage - it is surfaced only at low/informational severity. You may still want a dedicated subnet-level flow log if you need per-subnet configuration, such as a different log destination, aggregation interval, or retention for this subnet.
remediation: No action is required for coverage. If you need per-subnet control over flow log configuration or retention, create a subnet-level flow log for this subnet in addition to the VPC-level one.
